heading: Old Adaminaby vs Old Bonalbo — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Adaminaby, New South Wales and Old Bonalbo, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Old Bonalbo has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Old Adaminaby (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Adaminaby or Old Bonalbo safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Adaminaby has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Old Bonalbo scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Adaminaby scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Old Bonalbo scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Adaminaby or Old Bonalbo?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Old Bonalbo has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
